definitions.text: Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.
examples: The wind blew through her hair as she stood on the deck of the ship.
As they accelerated onto the motorway, the wind tore the plywood off the car's roof-rack.

definitions.text: Air artificially put in motion by any force or action.
examples: the wind of a cannon ball
After the second lap he was already out of wind.
The fall knocked the wind out of him.

definitions.text: To blow air through a wind instrument or horn to make a sound.
To cause (someone) to become breathless, as by a blow to the abdomen, or by physical exertion, running, etc.
examples: The boxer was winded during round two.

definitions.text: To cause a baby to bring up wind by patting its back after being fed.
To turn coils (of a cord or something similar) around something.
examples: to wind thread on a spool or into a ball

definitions.text: The act of winding or turning; a turn; a bend; a twist.
